vuln_points.description: Unbounded stack buffer overflow via gets()
evidence: Disasm context: 401252:	e8 79 fe ff ff       	call   4010d0 <gets@plt>
[Taint] Register analysis: Could not determine arg source — conservative PROBABLE
[Taint] Direct taint path found and register args appear user-controlled
vuln_points.mitigation: Replace gets() with fgets(buf, sizeof(buf), stdin). Compiler hardening: enable NX/DEP (-Wl,-z,noexecstack), compile with -fPIE -pie, enable stack canaries (-fstack-protector-strong), enable RELRO (-Wl,-z,relro,-z,now).

vuln_points.description: Uncontrolled format string — memory read/write primitive
evidence: Disasm context: 401205:	e8 b6 fe ff ff       	call   4010c0 <printf@plt>
[Taint] Register analysis: Could not determine arg source — conservative PROBABLE
[Taint] Direct taint path found and register args appear user-controlled
vuln_points.mitigation: Always pass a literal format string: printf("%s", user_input). Compiler hardening: enable NX/DEP (-Wl,-z,noexecstack), compile with -fPIE -pie, enable stack canaries (-fstack-protector-strong), enable RELRO (-Wl,-z,relro,-z,now).

vuln_points.description: Network recv() without size validation — heap overflow
evidence: Disasm context: 40123e:	e8 4d fe ff ff       	call   401090 <recv@plt>
Disasm context: 401243:	48 8d 85 70 ff ff ff 	lea    -0x90(%rbp),%rax
Disasm context: 401252:	e8 79 fe ff ff       	call   4010d0
[Taint] Register analysis: Could not determine arg source — conservative PROBABLE
[Taint] Direct taint path found and register args appear user-controlled
vuln_points.mitigation: Always pass sizeof(buf) as the length argument to recv(). Compiler hardening: enable NX/DEP (-Wl,-z,noexecstack), compile with -fPIE -pie, enable stack canaries (-fstack-protector-strong), enable RELRO (-Wl,-z,relro,-z,now).

evidence: Disasm context: 40129f:	e8 3c fe ff ff       	call   4010e0 <sprintf@plt>
Disasm context: 4012a4:	48 8b 85 f0 fe ff ff 	mov    -0x110(%rbp),%rax
[Taint] Register analysis: Could not determine arg source — conservative PROBABLE
[Taint] Direct taint path found and register args appear user-controlled
vuln_points.mitigation: Replace sprintf() with snprintf() with explicit size. Compiler hardening: enable NX/DEP (-Wl,-z,noexecstack), compile with -fPIE -pie, enable stack canaries (-fstack-protector-strong), enable RELRO (-Wl,-z,relro,-z,now).

vuln_points.description: Unchecked string copy — potential stack overflow
evidence: Disasm context: 4011f4:	e8 a7 fe ff ff       	call   4010a0 <strcpy@plt>
Disasm context: 401205:	e8 b6 fe ff ff       	call   4010c0
[Taint] Register analysis: Could not determine arg source — conservative PROBABLE
[Taint] Direct taint path found and register args appear user-controlled
vuln_points.mitigation: Replace strcpy() with strlcpy() or snprintf(). Compiler hardening: enable NX/DEP (-Wl,-z,noexecstack), compile with -fPIE -pie, enable stack canaries (-fstack-protector-strong), enable RELRO (-Wl,-z,relro,-z,now).

vuln_points.description: system() call — OS command injection if input reaches it
evidence: [Taint] Register analysis: Could not determine arg source — conservative PROBABLE
[Taint] Direct taint path found and register args appear user-controlled
vuln_points.mitigation: Avoid system(). Use execve() with a fixed path and validated args. Compiler hardening: enable NX/DEP (-Wl,-z,noexecstack), compile with -fPIE -pie, enable stack canaries (-fstack-protector-strong), enable RELRO (-Wl,-z,relro,-z,now).
